market_brief: VIX at 26.78 signals sustained elevated volatility with asymmetric skew favoring downside protection—premium sellers should demand wider strikes on short positions and consider rolling duration rather than adding fresh shorts. The 10Y at 4.391% combined with a strong DXY at 99.503 is suppressing risk appetite in high-multiple tech, evidenced by widespread beat_run failures across the Magnificent Seven and semis (MSFT, AAPL, QCOM, LRCX all sub-threshold), suggesting sector rotation into value and defensives will persist. DDOG's 54% confidence is marginal and insufficient for size—the single survivor with only 54% confidence in a 26+ VIX regime is a tell that the scanner is finding thin alpha today. Brent at $106.41 is providing a bid to energy/industrials but remains a tactical headwind for consumer cyclicals; stay long vol on tech weakness into any relief rallies rather than fighting macro. Key risk: if Treasury yields break lower on flight-to-safety, we could see a violent reversion in short volatility positioning, so monitor the 4.35% level on 10Y closely.
